page_content:
Introduction: Some Definations
March 4, 2026
Regression Analysis              S. Ghorai ( Dept. AEIE, HITK)
3
❑Correlation Analysis is the study of the relationship between variables. 
It is also defined as group of techniques to measure the association 
between two variables.
❑Scatter Diagram is a chart that portrays the relationship between the 
two variables. It is the usual first step in correlations analysis
❑The Dependent Variable is the variable being predicted or estimated.

metadata.creator: Microsoft® PowerPoint® 2019
metadata.title: Design Procedure by Impulse Invariant Method
metadata.author: Santanu

page_content:
❑The Independent Variable provides the basis for estimation.  It is the 
predictor variable.

Regression Analysis              S. Ghorai ( Dept. AEIE, HITK)
4
❑Is there an association between: 
✓Children’s IQ and Parents’ IQ
✓Degree of social trust and number of membership in voluntary 
association 
✓Urban growth and air quality violations 
✓Research assistantship funding and number of publication by Ph.D. 
students
✓Number of police patrol and number of crime 
✓Grade on exam and time on exam

page_content:
Scatter Plot:
March 4, 2026
Regression Analysis              S. Ghorai ( Dept. AEIE, HITK)
5
❑The relationship between any two variables can be portrayed graphically on an x- 
and y- axis.  
❑Each subject i1 has (x1, y1),  when scores for an entire sample are plotted, the 
result is called scatter plot. 
❑Example- Determine whether there is a relationship between the number of sales 
calls made in a month and the number of copiers sold  in that month.

page_content:
Direction of the Relationship:
March 4, 2026
Regression Analysis              S. Ghorai ( Dept. AEIE, HITK)
6
❑Variables can be positively or negatively correlated or no correlation: 
✓Positive correlation: A value of one variable increase, value of other 
variable increase. 
✓Negative correlation: A value of one variable increase, value of other 
variable decrease.

page_content:
Variance vs. Covariance:
March 4, 2026
Regression Analysis              S. Ghorai ( Dept. AEIE, HITK)
7
❑Do two variables change together?
❑Variance:
✓ Gives information on variability of a single variable.
❑Covariance:
✓Gives information on the degree to which two variables vary together.
✓Note how similar the covariance is to variance: the equation simply multiplies x’s 
error scores by y’s error scores as opposed to squaring x’s error scores.

page_content:
Variance vs. Covariance (Contd.):
March 4, 2026
Regression Analysis              S. Ghorai ( Dept. AEIE, HITK)
8
❑First, a note on your sample:  
✓If you’re wishing to assume that your sample is representative of the 
general population (RANDOM EFFECTS MODEL), use the degrees of 
freedom (n – 1) in your calculations of variance or covariance.
✓But if you’re simply wanting to assess your current sample (FIXED 
EFFECTS MODEL), substitute n for the degrees of freedom.

❑When X   and Y   : cov (x,y) = neg.
❑When no constant relationship: cov (x,y) = 0

page_content:
Problem with Covariance:
March 4, 2026
Regression Analysis              S. Ghorai ( Dept. AEIE, HITK)
11
❑The value obtained by covariance is dependent on the size of the 
data’s standard deviations: if large, the value will be greater than 
if small… even if the relationship between x and y is exactly the 
same in the large versus small standard deviation datasets.

Strength of the Relationship:
March 4, 2026
Regression Analysis              S. Ghorai ( Dept. AEIE, HITK)
15
❑The magnitude of correlation: 
✓Indicated by its numerical value
✓Ignoring the sign
✓Expresses the strength of the linear relationship between the variables.

page_content:
Pearson’s Correlation Coefficient:
March 4, 2026
Regression Analysis              S. Ghorai ( Dept. AEIE, HITK)
17
❑There are many kinds of correlation coefficients but the most commonly 
used measure of correlation is the Pearson’s correlation coefficient- (r)
✓The Pearson r range between -1 to +1.  
✓Sign indicate the direction.
✓The numerical value indicates the strength. 
✓Perfect correlation : -1 or 1 
✓No correlation: 0 
✓A correlation of zero indicates the value are not linearly related.

metadata.creator: Microsoft® PowerPoint® 2019
metadata.title: Design Procedure by Impulse Invariant Method
metadata.author: Santanu

page_content: ✓However, it is possible they are related in curvilinear fashion.

metadata.creator: Microsoft® PowerPoint® 2019
metadata.title: Design Procedure by Impulse Invariant Method
metadata.author: Santanu

page_content:
Pearson’s Correlation Coefficient:
March 4, 2026
Regression Analysis              S. Ghorai ( Dept. AEIE, HITK)
18
❑The Coefficient of Correlation (r) is a measure of the strength of the 
relationship between two variables.

metadata.creator: Microsoft® PowerPoint® 2019
metadata.title: Design Procedure by Impulse Invariant Method
metadata.author: Santanu

page_content:
Pearson’s Correlation Coefficient:
March 4, 2026
Regression Analysis              S. Ghorai ( Dept. AEIE, HITK)
19
❑EXAMPLE: Using the Copier Sales of America data  which a 
scatter plot is shown below, compute the correlation 
coefficient and coefficient of determination.
Using the formula:
❑How do we interpret a correlation of 0.759? 
❑First, it is positive, so we see there is a direct relationship between the 
number of sales calls and the number of copiers sold. The value of 0.759 is

metadata.creator: Microsoft® PowerPoint® 2019
metadata.title: Design Procedure by Impulse Invariant Method
metadata.author: Santanu

page_content:
fairly close to 1.00, so we conclude that the association is strong.  However, 
does this mean that more sales calls cause more sales?  No, we have not 
demonstrated cause and effect here, only that the two variables—sales 
calls and copiers sold—are related.

metadata.creator: Microsoft® PowerPoint® 2019
metadata.title: Design Procedure by Impulse Invariant Method
metadata.author: Santanu

page_content:
Testing the Significance of the Correlation Coefficient – Copier Sales Example:
March 4, 2026
Regression Analysis              S. Ghorai ( Dept. AEIE, HITK)
20
❑H0:  = 0 (the correlation in the population 
is 0)
❑H1:  ≠ 0 (the correlation in the population 
is not 0)
 Reject H0 if:
 t > t/2,n-2  or t < -t/2,n-2 
   t > t0.025,8 or t < -t0.025,8
   t > 2.306 or t < -2.306
❑The computed t (3.297) is within the rejection region, therefore, we will reject H0.

metadata.creator: Microsoft® PowerPoint® 2019
metadata.title: Design Procedure by Impulse Invariant Method
metadata.author: Santanu

page_content:
This means the correlation in the population is not zero. From a practical 
standpoint, it indicates to the sales manager that there is correlation with respect 
to the number of sales calls made and the number of copiers sold in the population 
of salespeople.

❑When r = 1 or r = -1:
✓We can predict y from x with certainty
✓all data points are on a straight line: y = ax + b
❑r is actually 
✓r = true r of whole population
✓  = estimate of r based on data
❑r is very sensitive to extreme values:

Regression:
March 4, 2026
Regression Analysis              S. Ghorai ( Dept. AEIE, HITK)
22
❑Correlation tells you if there is an association between x and y but it 
doesn’t describe the relationship or allow you to predict one variable from 
the other.
❑To do this we need REGRESSION!

metadata.creator: Microsoft® PowerPoint® 2019
metadata.title: Design Procedure by Impulse Invariant Method
metadata.author: Santanu

page_content:
Best Fit Line: Linear Regression
March 4, 2026
Regression Analysis              S. Ghorai ( Dept. AEIE, HITK)
23
❑Aim of linear regression is to fit a straight line, ŷ = ax + b, to data that 
gives best prediction of y for any value of x.
❑This will be the line that minimises distance between data and fitted line, 
i.e., the residuals.
ε
ŷ = ax + b
ε   =  residual error

page_content:
Method of Least Square Regression:
March 4, 2026
Regression Analysis              S. Ghorai ( Dept. AEIE, HITK)
24
❑To obtain the estimates of the coefficients ‘a’ and ‘b’, the least squares 
method minimizes the sum of squares of residuals. 
❑The residual for the ith data point ei is defined as the difference between 
the observed value of the response variable, yi, and the estimate of the 
response variable, ŷi, and is identified as the error associated with the 
data. i.e., ei = yi–ŷi , i =1 ,2, ..., n.

metadata.creator: Microsoft® PowerPoint® 2019
metadata.title: Design Procedure by Impulse Invariant Method
metadata.author: Santanu

page_content:
data. i.e., ei = yi–ŷi , i =1 ,2, ..., n.
❑The method of least squares helps us to find the values of unknowns ‘a’ and 
‘b’ in such a way that the following two conditions are satisfied:
❑Sum of the residuals is zero. That is
  
❑Sum of the squares of the residuals                                        is the least.

page_content:
Method of Least Square Regression:
March 4, 2026
Regression Analysis              S. Ghorai ( Dept. AEIE, HITK)
25
❑To find the best line we must minimise the sum of the squares of the 
residuals (the vertical distances from the data points to our line)
✓Residual (ε) = y - ŷ
✓Sum of squares of residuals E(a, b) = Σ (y – ŷ)2 
✓Model line: ŷ = ax + b
❑ We must find values of a and b that minimise: 
E(a, b)=Σ (y – ŷ)2 
a = slope, b = intercept

(high value of a)

❑From the Copier Sales of America problem, determine a 95 percent 
confidence interval for all sales representatives who make 25 calls. 
❑Step 1 – Compute the point estimate of Y-
     In other words, determine the number of copiers we expect a sales

✓To find the t value, we need to first know the number of degrees of 
freedom. In this case the degrees of freedom is n - 2 = 10 – 2 = 8. 
✓We set the confidence level at 95 percent. 
✓The value of t is 2.306.

❑Multivariate regression is used to determine the effect of a number of 
independent variables, x1, x2, x3, etc., on a single dependent variable, y.
❑The different x variables are combined in a linear way and each has its own 
regression coefficient:
  
❑The ‘a’ parameters reflect the independent contribution of each 
independent variable, x, to the value of the dependent variable, y.
